claim: The Dominican economy is not collapsing.
allowed_wording: The available evidence does not support a collapse narrative.
forbidden_wording: The economy is fully healthy; there is no serious pressure.
boundary: Rejects collapse only; does not prove comfort.

claim: The evidence does not support a comfort narrative.
allowed_wording: Macro resilience does not prove household comfort.
forbidden_wording: Households are definitely worse off across the board.
boundary: Rejects comfort only; does not prove broad hardship for every group.

claim: The best current interpretation is a selective / uneven squeeze.
allowed_wording: The evidence points to an uneven or selective squeeze rather than collapse or comfort.
forbidden_wording: The investigation proves universal household distress.
boundary: Interim synthesis; should remain tied to specific supported channels.

allowed_wording: Formal contributors show a wage-food offset channel.
forbidden_wording: Dominican households broadly offset food pressure.
boundary: Formal TSS contributors only; cannot generalize to informal workers or all households.

claim: Formal-worker cushioning generalizes to all households.
forbidden_wording: Formal wage gains prove household-wide cushioning.
boundary: Rejected because formal data does not cover informal workers or all households.

claim: Current informal-worker income offset is proven.
allowed_wording: Current informal-worker income offset remains unresolved.

claim: Current household-wide income offset is proven.
Official 2026 labor context supports current employment/labor-market context.
allowed_wording: Official 2026 labor data provides current labor-market context.
boundary: Does not lock current income/remuneration metric.

claim: Current labor context proves current income offset.
forbidden_wording: Employment context proves households had enough income to offset prices.
boundary: Rejected because labor/employment context is not equivalent to income/remuneration offset.

claim: Near-current 2025 FMCG behavior context is useful narrative context.
allowed_wording: Near-current 2025 FMCG evidence provides context about consumer pressure and behavior.
boundary: Context only; cannot prove current 2026 household trade-down.

forbidden_wording: Households traded down in 2026 because 2025 FMCG data suggests behavior pressure.

claim: Official 2025 BCRD informal-income pattern is useful near-current context.
allowed_wording: 2025 BCRD data shows the kind of informal-income metric needed for offset testing.
boundary: Near-current context only; stale for 2026 offset calculation.

forbidden_wording: 2025 informal income can be used to calculate 2026 offset.

claim: Current direct household trade-down is proven.
allowed_wording: Direct current household trade-down remains unproven.
boundary: Requires current direct behavior evidence: SKU, basket, brand switching, quantities, smaller units, skipped categories, or survey substitution data.

allowed_wording: The opposite claim is also unproven.
boundary: Absence in tested sources is not proof of absence globally.

forbidden_wording: Higher prices prove households switched brands or bought less.
boundary: Rejected because pressure does not equal behavior.

claim: Fiscal, credit, business, or macro proxies prove trade-down.
forbidden_wording: Business stress or weak demand proves household trade-down.
boundary: Rejected because proxy pressure does not prove direct household behavior.

claim: Global absence of sources is proven.
allowed_wording: No direct current trade-down evidence was locked in the tested sources.
boundary: Tested-source absence cannot become global absence.
